name: SvelteKit verification, docs, and dead-file cleanup
outcome: The rewritten server is verified end-to-end against the SvelteKit client, dead NestJS/Prisma files are removed, and docs reflect the Hono + Drizzle stack.
scope: manual web verification, dead-file/dependency cleanup, README + CLAUDE.md updates.
acceptance_criteria: The SvelteKit client completes login -> authorize -> callback against the rewritten server on port 3000

No @nestjs/*, @prisma/client, express, nunjucks, csrf-csrf, reflect-metadata, rxjs, class-validator/transformer remain; prisma/, src/logging/, src/main.ts, src/app.module.ts, old controllers/services, src/global.d.ts, and views/ are deleted
README and CLAUDE.md describe the Hono + Drizzle stack and commands
steps: Run web/ against the server and verify the login -> /callback round trip in a browser (tooling-agent-browser)
Delete dead files: prisma/, src/logging/, src/main.ts, src/app.module.ts, src/app.controller.ts, src/app/prisma/, the old oauth controllers/services, src/global.d.ts, and any remaining nunjucks views

tsconfig.json — remove the `./src/global.d.ts` entry from compilerOptions.types and drop the stale `prisma/**/*.ts` glob from include (prisma/ is deleted); then run pnpm build (tsc) and fix any residual type errors
README.md and CLAUDE.md — update setup/scripts (cp .env.example .env, tsx, drizzle-kit, vitest) and the stack description
notes: Final integration + cleanup slice; the dependency chain ends here. Keep seed IDs, /api routes, and port 3000 intact so the SvelteKit client keeps working. pnpm build (tsc) must pass under the modernized strict tsconfig from issue 1 (strict + verbatimModuleSyntax), so all new code is strict-clean and uses import type for type-only imports.
